content.problemStatement:
JJ gives Chef a number XXX and challenges Chef to come up with three **distinct** numbers A,A,A, B,B,B, and CCC such that:


0â‰¤A,B,C<2300 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30}0â‰¤A,B,C<230;
(AâŠ•B)+(BâŠ•C)+(CâŠ•A)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(AâŠ•B)+(BâŠ•C)+(CâŠ•A)=X.
Help Chef come up with three such numbers or determine that no such tuple exists.

Here, âŠ•\oplusâŠ• denotes the bitwise XOR operation.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer XXX - the number mentioned in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output three **distinct** numbers AAA, BBB and CCC (0â‰¤A,B,C<230)(0 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30})(0â‰¤A,B,C<230) such that (AâŠ•B)+(BâŠ•C)+(CâŠ•A)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(AâŠ•B)+(BâŠ•C)+(CâŠ•A)=X.


If multiple such tuples exist, print any. If no such tuple exists, print âˆ’1-1âˆ’1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single integer X (0 \u2264 X < 2^30).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three integers A, B, C (0 \u2264 A, B, C < 2^30) such that (A\u2295B)+(B\u2295C)+(C\u2295A)=X. If no such triple exists output "-1 -1 -1". Any valid triple is accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation: X=6 is even, Y=3 â†’ (0,3,3) gives 3+0+3=6. X=3 is odd â†’ impossible. X=20 is even, Y=10 â†’ (0,10,10) gives 10+0+10=20.
X=1 is odd, no triple can satisfy the condition.

solution.approach: The expression (A\u2295B)+(B\u2295C)+(C\u2295A) can be satisfied very easily. Choose A=0 and B=C=Y. Then (0\u2295Y)=Y, (Y\u2295Y)=0 and (Y\u22950)=Y, so the sum becomes Y+0+Y=2Y. Therefore any even X can be represented by setting Y=X/2 and outputting (0,Y,Y). If X is odd the sum 2Y can never equal X, so no solution exists. This construction also works for X=0 (Y=0). The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

code.C: #include <stdio.h>\nint main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long X; if(scanf("%lld",&X)!=1) return 0; if(X & 1LL) printf("-1 -1 -1\\n"); else{ long long Y = X/2; printf("0 %lld %lld\\n",Y,Y); } } return 0; }
code.C++: #include <bits/stdc++.h>\nusing namespace std;\nint main(){ios::sync_with_stdio(false);cin.tie(nullptr);int T; if(!(cin>>T)) return 0; while(T--){ long long X; cin>>X; if(X & 1LL) cout<<"-1 -1 -1\\n"; else{ long long Y = X/2; cout<<0<<' '<<Y<<' '<<Y<<"\\n"; } } return 0; }
code.Java: import java.io.*;\nimport java.util.*;\npublic class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){ long X=Long.parseLong(br.readLine().trim()); if((X & 1L)==1L) sb.append("-1 -1 -1\\n"); else{ long Y=X/2; sb.append("0 ").append(Y).append(' ').append(Y).append('\\n'); } }System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data: return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        X=int(next(it))
        if X & 1:
            out.append("-1 -1 -1")
        else:
            Y=X//2
            out.append(f"0 {Y} {Y}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))
if __name__=="__main__":
    solve()
